# Project Catalog (110 projects)

---

## NousResearch/hermes-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 83,290
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
The self-improving AI agent that grows with you — persistent memory, auto-generated skills, 14 platforms, 6 execution backends

Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent, cross-platform task execution and self-improvement. It utilizes a closed learning loop to generate new skills from experience, curate long-term memory, and build user models across sessions. The agent integrates with 14 messaging platforms and supports six execution backends, including serverless environments like Modal and Daytona that hibernate when idle. Users can swap between numerous LLM providers and utilize a built-in cron scheduler for natural language automations.

Highlights:
- Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement from task experience
- Multi-platform support including Telegram, Discord, Slack, and CLI
- Flexible execution across local, Docker, SSH, and serverless backends

---

## mem0ai/mem0

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/mem0ai/mem0
GitHub: https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 53,915
Universal memory layer for AI Agents — official Hermes Agent memory provider, available as managed cloud or Apache-2.0 self-hosted OSS

Mem0 is a universal memory layer designed to provide AI agents and assistants with long-term persistence and personalization. It utilizes a token-efficient algorithm that combines single-pass fact extraction with multi-signal retrieval, fusing semantic, keyword, and entity matching. The system manages state across users, sessions, and agents while incorporating temporal reasoning to distinguish between past events and current preferences. It is available as an Apache-2.0 self-hosted server, a Python/Node.js library, or a managed cloud platform.

Highlights:
- Multi-level memory retention for users, sessions, and agent states
- Hybrid retrieval using semantic, BM25 keyword, and entity linking
- Temporal reasoning for time-aware ranking of past and future events

---

## garrytan/gbrain

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/garrytan/gbrain
GitHub: https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 8,857
Garry's Opinionated OpenClaw/Hermes Agent Brain

GBrain is a persistent memory and knowledge graph system designed to prevent AI agents from losing context over time. It utilizes a self-wiring graph layer and hybrid search to extract entity relationships like investments and affiliations without requiring additional LLM calls. The system consolidates data from meetings, emails, and tweets into a structured timeline with backlink-boosted ranking for improved retrieval accuracy. It supports 14 embedding providers and includes built-in benchmarking tools to evaluate retrieval performance against standard datasets.

Highlights:
- Self-wiring knowledge graph extracts entity relationships without LLM calls.
- Hybrid search combines vector retrieval with backlink-boosted graph ranking.
- Supports 14 embedding providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and local Ollama.

---

## vectorize-io/hindsight

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/vectorize-io/hindsight
GitHub: https://github.com/vectorize-io/hindsight
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 8,362
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Agent memory that learns — long-term retain/recall/reflect workflows

Hindsight is an agent memory system designed to enable long-term learning and information retention for AI agents. It utilizes a workflow of retain, recall, and reflect operations to move beyond simple conversation history into persistent mental models. The system can be integrated via a two-line LLM wrapper or a direct API to automate memory storage and retrieval during LLM calls. It supports multi-user personalization through metadata filtering and is compatible with major providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.

Highlights:
- Implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent learning
- Offers a two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory management
- Supports PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database for enterprise-grade storage

---

## mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
GitHub: https://github.com/mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 4,132
754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, ATLAS, D3FEND & NIST AI RMF

Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills is an open-source library of 754 structured skills designed to provide AI agents with the domain-specific workflows of a senior security analyst. Each skill follows the agentskills.io standard, utilizing YAML frontmatter and structured Markdown to guide agents through step-by-step execution and verification. The repository maps every skill across five major frameworks, including MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, and the NIST AI RMF. It is compatible with over 26 platforms, including Claude Code and Hermes Agent, to support automated threat hunting, digital forensics, and cloud security operations.

Highlights:
- 754 structured skills across 26 specialized security domains
- Unified mapping to MITRE ATT&CK, ATLAS, D3FEND, and NIST frameworks
- Standardized agentskills.io format for sub-second discovery and execution

---

## builderz-labs/mission-control

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/builderz-labs/mission-control
GitHub: https://github.com/builderz-labs/mission-control
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 3,875
Self-hosted AI agent orchestration — dispatch tasks, run multi-agent workflows, monitor spend

Mission Control is a self-hosted dashboard designed to orchestrate and monitor fleets of AI agents. It utilizes a local-first architecture powered by SQLite and Next.js to coordinate multi-agent workflows, dispatch tasks, and track operational costs without external database dependencies. The platform features built-in quality gates for task approval, real-time telemetry via WebSockets, and a security framework that includes secret detection and trust scoring. It integrates with various agent frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph, and provides a bridge for CLI tools such as Claude Code.

Highlights:
- Self-hosted orchestration using SQLite with zero external dependencies
- Multi-gateway support for CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen, and Claude SDK
- Built-in Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off

---

## plastic-labs/honcho

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/plastic-labs/honcho
GitHub: https://github.com/plastic-labs/honcho
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 3,143
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Memory library for building stateful agents — the upstream library that the elkimek/honcho-self-hosted Hermes wrapper builds on.

Honcho is a memory infrastructure library designed to build stateful agents that track evolving relationships between users, groups, and projects. It utilizes a background reasoning loop to extract conclusions and peer representations from stored conversation logs and events rather than relying solely on simple vector matching. Developers can query these representations for natural-language insights or inject session-specific context directly into LLM prompts. The system supports both a managed cloud service and a self-hosted FastAPI server with integrations for MCP, Claude Code, and Hermes.

Highlights:
- Asynchronous reasoning extracts conclusions from messages and event traces
- Peer-centric model tracks evolving entities and multi-peer perspectives
- Hybrid search combines BM25 and vector retrieval for context

---

## nesquena/hermes-webui

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/nesquena/hermes-webui
GitHub: https://github.com/nesquena/hermes-webui
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 2,479
Hermes WebUI: The best way to use Hermes Agent from the web or from your phone!

Hermes WebUI is a lightweight, browser-based interface for the Hermes Agent, providing a graphical alternative to the standard command-line experience. Built with Python and vanilla JavaScript, it maintains full parity with CLI features without requiring complex build steps or frameworks. The interface features a three-panel layout for managing chat sessions, workspace files, and agent settings through a centralized control center. It allows users to interact with persistent agent memory, manage self-improving skills, and monitor token usage via a visual context ring.

Highlights:
- Full feature parity with the Hermes Agent CLI experience
- Three-panel layout for chat, file browsing, and session management
- Zero-build architecture using only Python and vanilla JavaScript

---

## EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui
GitHub: https://github.com/EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 1,844
Web dashboard for Hermes Agent — multi-platform AI chat, session management, scheduled jobs, usage analytics & channel configuration (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp)

Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive management dashboard for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that provides a graphical interface for AI interaction and system administration. The platform connects directly to the Hermes state database and configuration files to manage chat sessions, provider credentials, and multi-platform communication channels. Users can monitor real-time usage analytics, schedule automated cron jobs, and manage files across local or remote backends like Docker and SSH. It also features a multi-agent group chat system with @mention routing and automated context compression for long conversations.

Highlights:
- Unified configuration for eight platforms including Telegram, Discord, and Slack
- Real-time usage analytics with token tracking and cost estimation
- Integrated file browser and web terminal for remote backend management

## junhoyeo/tokscale

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/junhoyeo/tokscale
GitHub: https://github.com/junhoyeo/tokscale
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 1,690
CLI tool for tracking token usage from Claude Code, OpenClaw, Hermes, Codex, Gemini, and more

Tokscale is a CLI tool and visualization dashboard designed to track token consumption and costs across various AI coding agents. It works by scanning local databases and session files from supported clients to aggregate usage data into a native Rust TUI or a web-based 3D contribution graph. The tool provides detailed daily summaries and model-specific statistics for a wide range of integrations, including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and GitHub Copilot CLI. Users can also submit their data to a global leaderboard to create public usage profiles.

Highlights:
- Tracks token usage and costs across multiple AI coding agents
- Features a native Rust TUI for local data visualization
- Supports Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Gemini, and Cursor IDE

---

## NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 1,249
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Function calling examples and training data for Hermes LLM models

This repository provides the implementation and examples for enabling function calling and JSON mode in Hermes 2 Pro models. It utilizes a recursive loop to generate, execute, and process tool calls based on provided schemas, primarily using the ChatML prompt format for structured dialogue. Users can define custom tools via Python functions or enforce specific output structures using Pydantic models. The included examples demonstrate practical applications in financial data retrieval, such as fetching stock prices and fundamentals through the yfinance library.

Highlights:
- Enables recursive function calling and tool execution loops
- Supports structured JSON mode using Pydantic schema validation
- Uses ChatML format for OpenAI-compatible multi-turn interactions

---

## AMAP-ML/SkillClaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AMAP-ML/SkillClaw
GitHub: https://github.com/AMAP-ML/SkillClaw
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 1,103
Let Skills Evolve Collectively with Agentic Evolver

SkillClaw is an agentic skill management system that enables AI agents to evolve their capabilities through real-world interactions. It operates as a background process that automatically deduplicates, improves, and merges skills across different sessions and devices. The system creates a unified skill library that synchronizes experience between multiple agents and users, allowing knowledge gained in one context to benefit others. It natively integrates with Hermes, OpenClaw, and various OpenAI-compatible APIs to provide a continuous evolution loop for agentic tools.

Highlights:
- Automates skill deduplication and quality improvement across agent sessions
- Unifies skill libraries across multiple devices and different agent frameworks
- Enables collective evolution by sharing learned skills within user groups

---

## NousResearch/atropos

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/atropos
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/atropos
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 997
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
RL training environments framework for tool-calling models

Atropos is an environment microservice framework designed for asynchronous reinforcement learning (RL) with large language models. It operates by setting up interactive environments as services that communicate via a trajectory API to collect and distribute training data for external trainers. The system supports a wide range of tasks including multi-turn tool calling, code execution, and multimodal evaluation. Research using this framework has demonstrated performance gains in complex domains such as parallel function calling and financial prediction.

Highlights:
- Async microservice architecture for scalable RL environment management
- Trajectory API for standardized data collection and trainer integration
- Supports diverse environments including tool calling, coding, and RLAIF

---

## numtide/llm-agents.nix

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/numtide/llm-agents.nix
GitHub: https://github.com/numtide/llm-agents.nix
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 967
Nix packages for AI coding agents including Hermes

llm-agents.nix is a collection of Nix packages designed to simplify the deployment and management of AI coding agents and development tools. The project provides a centralized repository of Nix expressions that automate the installation of various agentic tools, with updates generated daily to ensure compatibility. It enables developers to run a wide range of CLI-based agents, including Claude Code, Goose, and Bernstein, directly through the Nix package manager without manual environment configuration. This infrastructure supports diverse use cases from multi-agent orchestration to terminal-based pair programming across different LLM providers.

Highlights:
- Provides daily updated Nix packages for AI coding agents
- Supports major tools like Claude Code, Goose, and Cursor Agent
- Enables direct execution via Nix run without manual installation

---

Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 898
Curated list of awesome skills, tools, integrations, and resources for Hermes Agent

Awesome Hermes Agent is a community-curated repository that tracks the ecosystem of skills, tools, and integrations for the Hermes Agent framework. It provides a structured directory of resources including multi-platform messaging bridges, terminal backends, and autonomous skill-management utilities. The list helps developers navigate official Nous Research projects and community-contributed plugins for task orchestration, deployment, and self-evolution. Users can find specific blueprints for setting up GUIs, literate programming environments, and multi-agent swarms.

Highlights:
- Directory of skills for Hermes Agent's autonomous learning loop
- Guides for 18 messaging platforms and seven terminal backends
- Categorized resources for deployment, multi-agent swarms, and domain applications

## outsourc-e/hermes-workspace

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/outsourc-e/hermes-workspace
GitHub: https://github.com/outsourc-e/hermes-workspace
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 830
Native web workspace — chat, terminal, memory browser, skills manager, and inspector panel

Hermes Workspace is a comprehensive native web interface designed to serve as a command center for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It integrates chat, terminal access, memory management, and skill orchestration into a single dashboard to streamline agent interactions. The system operates as a zero-fork frontend that connects to vanilla Hermes Agent installations via API, supporting multi-agent swarms and persistent tmux-backed workers. Users can manage complex missions, browse thousands of skills, and monitor agent state through a unified control plane.

Highlights:
- Unified dashboard for chat, memory browsing, and terminal operations.
- Swarm Mode for role-based dispatch of multiple persistent agents.
- Zero-fork integration with vanilla NousResearch Hermes Agent installations.

---

## NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 778
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Evolutionary self-improvement using DSPy + GEPA — optimizes skills, prompts, and code

Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is an automated optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes Agent without requiring GPU training. It utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and mutate skill files, tool descriptions, and system prompts based on reflective feedback. The system validates candidate variants through constraint gates, including size limits and full test suite passes, before proposing improvements via pull requests. It supports evaluation using both synthetic data and real session history from tools like Claude Code and Copilot.

Highlights:
- Optimizes agent skills and prompts using DSPy and GEPA engines
- Operates via API calls without requiring local GPU resources
- Enforces strict guardrails including size limits and 100% test pass rates

---

## conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing
GitHub: https://github.com/conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 759
Audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI writing patterns

Avoid-ai-writing is a portable writing skill designed to audit and rewrite text to eliminate detectable AI patterns and "AI-isms." It utilizes a structured two-pass process that first identifies issues like significance inflation and copula avoidance, then performs a second audit to catch lingering tells. The tool relies on a 109-entry word replacement table and 36 specific pattern categories to ensure edits are grounded in plain language rather than general prompts. It is compatible with Hermes, Claude Code, and OpenClaw, offering both a full rewrite mode and a detection-only mode for auditing content.

Highlights:
- Identifies 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells
- Uses a three-tier, 109-entry word replacement table for consistency
- Performs a two-pass audit to catch recycled transitions and inflation

---

## greyhaven-ai/autocontext

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/greyhaven-ai/autocontext
GitHub: https://github.com/greyhaven-ai/autocontext
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 711
Recursive self-improving context harness — helps agents succeed on complex tasks

Autocontext is a recursive self-improving harness designed to help AI agents succeed on complex tasks through iterative evaluation. It works by running a multi-role loop—including competitors, analysts, and coaches—that tests strategies against real-world scenarios and retains successful outcomes. The system generates structured traces, playbooks, and datasets that allow future agent iterations to inherit accumulated knowledge. It integrates with various providers like Anthropic and OpenAI, and offers specialized support for Hermes Agent and MCP-compatible clients.

Highlights:
- Iterative self-improvement loop using five specialized agent roles
- Generates structured playbooks and traces for knowledge persistence
- Supports Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and multiple LLM providers

---

## NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 663
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Run Hermes as a managed employee in Paperclip company systems

The hermes-paperclip-adapter is a bridge that allows Hermes Agent to function as a managed employee within the Paperclip company ecosystem. It works by wrapping the Hermes CLI to parse raw terminal output into structured transcript entries, enabling Paperclip to render interactive tool cards and status icons. The adapter manages session persistence across heartbeats, allowing agents to maintain context while responding to task assignments or issue comments. It also synchronizes skills between local directories and the Paperclip interface while reclassifying system logs to ensure a clean user experience.

Highlights:
- Integrates 30+ native tools and 80+ skills into Paperclip
- Parses raw CLI output into structured markdown and tool cards
- Supports eight inference providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and OpenRouter

---

## wondelai/skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/wondelai/skills
GitHub: https://github.com/wondelai/skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 480
Cross-platform skills library for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents

Wondel.ai Skills is a cross-platform library of agent capabilities designed for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible environments. It provides pre-configured skill collections based on established industry frameworks, such as Jobs to Be Done, Clean Code, and Lean Startup methodology. Users can install these skills via the Claude Code marketplace or a dedicated CLI to equip AI agents with specialized knowledge in software architecture, UX design, and product strategy. The project bridges the gap between raw LLM capabilities and structured professional methodologies through modular, installable plugins.

Highlights:
- Compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents
- Modular installation via marketplace or npx skills CLI
- Frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy

---

## NousResearch/autonovel

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/autonovel
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/autonovel
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 401
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Autonomous novel-writing pipeline generating 100k+ word manuscripts

Autonovel is an autonomous agent pipeline designed to transform a seed concept into a complete, publishable novel. It utilizes a multi-phase workflow that includes world-building, sequential drafting, and an adversarial revision loop involving dual-persona LLM reviewers. The system integrates mechanical slop detection and voice fingerprinting to ensure prose quality and narrative consistency across tens of thousands of words. Beyond text generation, the project automates typesetting in LaTeX, cover art creation via fal.ai, and multi-voice audiobook production using ElevenLabs.

Highlights:
- Autonomous four-phase pipeline from world-building to final export
- Adversarial revision loop with mechanical and LLM-based evaluation
- Automated typesetting, cover illustration, and multi-voice audiobook generation

---

## xaspx/hermes-control-interface

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/xaspx/hermes-control-interface
GitHub: https://github.com/xaspx/hermes-control-interface
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 323
A self-hosted web dashboard for the Hermes AI agent stack. Provides a browser-based terminal, file explorer, session overview, cron management, system metrics, and an agent status panel — all behind a

The Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to manage the Hermes AI agent stack through a centralized graphical interface. It utilizes a Node.js and Express backend with WebSockets to provide real-time terminal access, multi-agent gateway management, and live log streaming. Users can configure over 80 agent settings, monitor token analytics and costs, and manage scheduled cron jobs across multiple profiles. The platform also includes a secure file explorer, a revamped chat interface with tool-call visualization, and robust role-based access control.

Highlights:
- Multi-agent gateway for starting, stopping, and configuring individual profiles.
- Real-time token analytics tracking usage, costs, and model performance.
- Integrated browser-based terminal and split-view file editor with syntax highlighting.

Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a focused workbench for managing Hermes Agent hosts. It connects directly via SSH to interact with remote sessions, files, and workflows without requiring a browser wrapper or local data synchronization. Users can manage Kanban boards, edit skills, and monitor usage across multiple agent profiles from a single interface. The app also includes an embedded terminal and local workflow presets to launch tasks directly on the remote host.

GitHub: https://github.com/swarmclawai/swarmclaw
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 285
Build autonomous AI agent swarms with orchestration, skills, and multiple model providers

SwarmClaw is a self-hosted runtime and framework designed for orchestrating autonomous multi-agent swarms. It operates by managing agent lifecycles through heartbeats, schedules, and delegation while providing durable memory and runtime skill learning. The platform supports over 23 LLM providers including Hermes, OpenAI, and Ollama, and can integrate with external CLI tools like Claude Code. Users can monitor activity through an organizational chart interface and deploy the system via Docker, desktop applications, or NPM.

Highlights:
- Supports 23+ LLM providers including Hermes, Ollama, and OpenRouter
- Features durable agent memory and conversation-to-skill learning
- Orchestrates swarms with delegation, schedules, and heartbeats

## ksimback/hermes-ecosystem

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ksimback/hermes-ecosystem
GitHub: https://github.com/ksimback/hermes-ecosystem
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 263
Hermes Atlas — the community map of every tool, skill, and integration for Hermes Agent by Nous Research

Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub star counts and growth trends cached via Redis.

Highlights:
- Directory of 80+ security-reviewed Hermes Agent tools and integrations
- RAG chatbot providing grounded answers from 27 research files

## clawshell/clawshell

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/clawshell/clawshell
GitHub: https://github.com/clawshell/clawshell
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 255
The Runtime Security Layer for OpenClaw/Hermes-agent, the essential safety harness for PII & sensitive credentials protection.

ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provides secure email isolation through IMAP sender filtering and supports OAuth-based authentication for providers like OpenAI.

Highlights:
- Maps virtual keys to real credentials stored in protected Unix directories
- Scans and redacts PII in request and response bodies via regex
- Enforces sender-based email filtering for secure mailbox access

---

## DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills
GitHub: https://github.com/DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 220
Type-safe agentskills.io support with progressive disclosure for Pydantic AI

pydantic-ai-skills is a standardized framework for managing modular agent capabilities within the Pydantic AI ecosystem. It implements the Agent Skills open specification by providing a tool-calling mechanism that allows agents to discover and execute specialized scripts or instructions. The library uses a progressive disclosure approach, loading skill details only when relevant to minimize token consumption. Developers can define these skills through local filesystem directories or programmatically using Python decorators and dataclasses.

Highlights:
- Implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI
- Reduces token usage through progressive disclosure of skill metadata
- Supports both filesystem-based and programmatic Python skill definitions

---

## ucsandman/DashClaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ucsandman/DashClaw
GitHub: https://github.com/ucsandman/DashClaw
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 204
Decision infrastructure with guard policies and risk assessment for agents

DashClaw is a governance and decision infrastructure layer designed to secure AI agents interacting with real-world systems. It intercepts risky agent actions to evaluate them against declarative policies, routing requests for human approval when risk thresholds are met. The system records verifiable evidence for every decision and tracks terminal outcomes to prevent silent double-execution during retries. It integrates with various runtimes including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and LangChain via MCP servers, SDKs, or lifecycle hooks.

Highlights:
- Evaluates agent actions against risk thresholds and declarative policies
- Provides human-in-the-loop approval queues via CLI, mobile, or messaging apps
- Prevents double-execution by tracking durable terminal outcomes and session states

---

Stars: 194
React SPA dashboard for Telegram Mini App v2.0 — 10-page mobile-first UI. Runs locally.

Hermes Telegram Mini App is a React-based terminal interface that allows users to manage and interact with Hermes agents directly within the Telegram ecosystem. It establishes a secure mobile-first workspace by wrapping the Hermes CLI in a dark-mode web UI, featuring streaming chat and system monitoring. The application utilizes dual HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 validation to ensure secure access while providing tools for cron job management and background agent spawning. Users can also perform vision-based tasks through integrated OCR and local LLM server support for file attachments.

Highlights:
- Terminal-style chat with streaming responses and slash command support
- Real-time system monitoring for CPU, memory, and disk usage
- Background agent spawning with support for five concurrent instances

## stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm
GitHub: https://github.com/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 178
Lossless Context Management plugin for Hermes Agent — DAG-based context engine that never loses a message

Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for the Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard lossy compression with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that compacts older messages into depth-aware summary nodes stored in a local SQLite database. This architecture allows the agent to maintain a bounded active context while using specialized tools to drill back into raw message history or specific summary branches. The system also includes automated controls for externalizing large payloads and media to prevent context flooding.

Highlights:
- Hierarchical DAG structure for depth-aware conversation compaction
- SQLite-backed message store for persistent raw history retrieval
- Agent tools for grepping, describing, and expanding compacted context

---

## 335234131/agent-browser-mcp

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/335234131/agent-browser-mcp
GitHub: https://github.com/335234131/agent-browser-mcp
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 167
让 Agent 直接操作真实 Chrome 的 MCP 服务，支持页面扫描、CDP、截图与物理输入

Agent-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that enables AI agents to interact directly with a user's active Chrome browser session rather than a sandboxed environment. It works through a local bridge and a Chrome extension to maintain existing login states, cookies, and open tabs while providing low-level control. The project allows agents to perform page scanning, execute JavaScript, and issue Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) commands. It also supports physical hardware interaction, including real mouse movements, keyboard inputs, and desktop screenshots.

Highlights:
- Operates real Chrome sessions with preserved login states and cookies
- Executes native CDP commands for advanced DOM and browser control
- Supports physical mouse and keyboard inputs for human-like automation

---

Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 131
Generate draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions

The drawio-skill project is a tool that converts natural-language descriptions into professional diagrams using the .drawio XML format. It utilizes the native draw.io desktop CLI to generate and export diagrams into PNG, SVG, PDF, or JPG formats. The system features six specialized presets for architecture, machine learning, and UML, while incorporating a multi-round self-check and feedback loop to refine visual accuracy. It integrates with various AI agents including Hermes, Cursor, and Claude Code to automate technical documentation workflows.

Highlights:
- Generates .drawio XML and exports to PNG, SVG, PDF, or JPG
- Includes presets for ERD, UML, Architecture, and ML diagrams
- Features automated self-check and iterative feedback refinement loops

## elkimek/honcho-self-hosted

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/elkimek/honcho-self-hosted
GitHub: https://github.com/elkimek/honcho-self-hosted
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 126
Self-hosted Honcho memory backend for cross-session persistence

This project provides a configuration layer for self-hosting Plastic Labs' Honcho memory backend, enabling cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent without relying on third-party cloud storage. It deploys the full Honcho stack—including the API, PostgreSQL with pgvector, and Redis—on local hardware using Docker Compose. Users can route memory extraction and reasoning tasks through any OpenAI-compatible API or local inference servers like Ollama to maintain data sovereignty. This setup allows Hermes to build long-term user profiles and logical observations while keeping conversation data on the user's own infrastructure.

Highlights:
- Enables private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent via self-hosting
- Supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider or local inference servers
- Includes automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis

## Cranot/super-hermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Cranot/super-hermes
GitHub: https://github.com/Cranot/super-hermes
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 122
Skills that teach Hermes Agent to write its own analytical prompts

Super Hermes is a skill registry for the Hermes Agent that enables models to generate their own analytical frameworks, or prisms, for deep code and document inspection. It works by instructing the agent to write specific thinking instructions before execution, allowing it to derive structural trade-offs and conservation laws rather than just identifying surface-level bugs. The system includes specialized commands like /prism-reflect, which provide a constraint report detailing what the analysis missed to ensure transparency in the agent's cognitive limits.

Highlights:
- Teaches agents to write custom thinking instructions for specific problems
- Identifies structural conservation laws and trade-offs in complex codebases
- Generates constraint reports detailing analysis blind spots and limitations

---

## AaronWong1999/hermesclaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AaronWong1999/hermesclaw
GitHub: https://github.com/AaronWong1999/hermesclaw
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 112
Run Hermes Agent and OpenClaw on the same WeChat account

HermesClaw is an integration bridge that allows Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and OpenCode to operate simultaneously on a single WeChat account. It acts as a central iLink poller and routes messages through local proxy servers to prevent the 403 errors and connection drops caused by multiple gateways competing for the same API token. This architecture enables users to switch between different AI models and agents using simple chat commands like /hermes, /openclaw, or /three. The tool also supports OpenCode's ACP bridge, allowing for voice-activated coding tasks directly within the WeChat interface.

Highlights:
- Enables three AI agents on one WeChat account simultaneously
- Prevents iLink token conflicts using a central proxy architecture
- Supports agent switching via /hermes, /openclaw, and /opencode commands

---

## yoloshii/ClawMem

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/yoloshii/ClawMem
GitHub: https://github.com/yoloshii/ClawMem
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 86
On-device memory and context engine for agents — local-first, no cloud dependencies

ClawMem is a local-first memory and context engine that provides AI agents with persistent, on-device knowledge. It utilizes a hybrid architecture combining multi-signal retrieval, intent classification, and multi-graph traversal to surface relevant context from markdown notes and session transcripts. The system integrates with Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes agents via a shared SQLite vault. It enables agents to capture decisions, generate session handoffs, and maintain a self-evolving memory layer without cloud dependencies.

Highlights:
- Local-first architecture with no API keys or cloud dependencies
- Hybrid retrieval using BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs
- Integrates via MCP server, Claude Code hooks, and agent plugins

## smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills

GitHub: https://github.com/smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 85
Oracle network and smart contract interaction skills (agentskills.io spec)

Chainlink Agent Skills is a collection of standardized coding tools designed to help AI agents interact with Chainlink's oracle network and smart contract services. The project implements the Agent Skills specification, allowing developers to equip agents with specialized knowledge for specific blockchain workflows via a CLI-based installation. These skills enable agents to perform tasks such as managing Verifiable Random Function (VRF) subscriptions, executing Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol (CCIP) transfers, and integrating real-time data feeds. By providing structured access to SDKs and contract addresses, it simplifies the creation of complex decentralized applications like tokenized funds and cross-chain settlement systems.

Highlights:
- Implements standardized Agent Skills specification for AI agent integration
- Supports CCIP, Data Feeds, VRF, and Data Streams workflows
- Provides CLI tools for local CRE simulations and cross-chain monitoring

## EfficientContext/ContextPilot

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/EfficientContext/ContextPilot
GitHub: https://github.com/EfficientContext/ContextPilot
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 82
Accelerating Long Context LLM Inference with Accuracy-Preserving Context Optimization in SGLang, vLLM, llama.cpp, OpenClaw, RAG, and Agentic AI.

ContextPilot is a context optimization engine designed to accelerate long-context LLM inference by maximizing prefix cache reuse. It functions by maintaining a context index that reorders and deduplicates overlapping data blocks before they reach the inference server. This approach reduces redundant KV cache recomputation and lowers token consumption without compromising reasoning accuracy. The project provides native plugins for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, and supports backends like vLLM, SGLang, and llama.cpp.

Highlights:
- Reduces prompt tokens by up to 36% through context deduplication
- Boosts prefill speeds by 1.5–3× via optimized prefix sharing
- Native integration for Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and vLLM

---

## tlehman/litprog-skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/tlehman/litprog-skill
GitHub: https://github.com/tlehman/litprog-skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 81
Literate programming skill — weave code and documentation across agents

The litprog-skill is a Claude Code extension that implements Donald Knuth's literate programming paradigm by transforming codebases into human-readable narratives. It works by generating a .lit.md file that weaves together prose, Mermaid diagrams, and LaTeX math with the original source code. The tool includes a tangler to extract runnable files and a reverse-sync engine to ensure the documentation stays updated when source files are edited. This approach helps developers document architectural decisions and facilitates easier onboarding through structured, essay-like code explanations.

Highlights:
- Generates .lit.md files combining prose, diagrams, and source code
- Extracts runnable source files from documentation using a tangler
- Automatically syncs source file edits back to the literate document

---

Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 81
Hermes Agent made portable desktop for Windows — 100 tools, GUI, local models via LM Studio, TTS, Music, ComfyUI, workflows, tool maker. No install. No Docker. No admin rights.

Portable Hermes Agent is a standalone Windows desktop application that provides a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring Docker, administrator rights, or system-wide installations. It operates through an embedded Python environment and connects to local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The platform integrates over 100 tools for tasks including image generation via ComfyUI, text-to-speech, music creation, and automated multi-step workflows. Users can also extend the agent's capabilities at runtime using a built-in dynamic tool maker for API wrappers and Python handlers.

Highlights:
- Zero-install Windows desktop GUI with granular permission controls
- Over 100 tools including TTS, MusicGen, and ComfyUI integrations
- Dynamic tool maker for creating API wrappers at runtime

## joeynyc/hermes-skins

GitHub: https://github.com/joeynyc/hermes-skins
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 76
Community CLI skins and themes for Hermes terminal UI

Hermes Skins is a collection of community-contributed visual themes for the Hermes CLI agent. It allows users to customize the terminal interface by modifying YAML configuration files that control banner colors, ASCII art, spinner animations, and branding text. The system uses a fallback mechanism where custom skins inherit missing values from a default theme, ensuring visual consistency while allowing for granular adjustments. Available themes range from retro computing aesthetics like MS-DOS and Vault-Tec to modern styles like Catppuccin and Synthwave.

Highlights:
- Customizes terminal visuals including ASCII banners and spinner animations
- Uses YAML configuration files for easy theme creation and sharing
- Inherits from default skin to support partial configuration overrides

The curriculum guides developers through implementing a core agent loop, tool registration, session persistence with SQLite, and context compression. It covers advanced infrastructure including multi-platform gateways for Telegram and Discord, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, and RL-based self-evolution. The project provides runnable reference implementations for each stage to help users understand the design backbone of autonomous systems.

Process monitoring and configuration dashboard for Hermes

Hermes WebUI is a dedicated process monitoring and configuration dashboard for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It functions as a non-intrusive polling bridge that reads from the agent's SQLite database and YAML files without requiring modifications to the core agent code. Users can track real-time activity, inspect full conversation histories via full-text search, and manage scheduled cron jobs through a responsive React-based interface. The tool also provides read-only visibility into system configurations, environment variables, and available skill source code.

- Full-text search and inspection of conversation histories and message details

hermes-CCC is a port of the Hermes Agent framework specifically designed for the Claude Code native skill system. It integrates the Hermes brain architecture directly into the Claude Code execution context, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth authentication. The project implements 46 core skills across categories such as MLOps, software development, and research, including specialized tools for RL training and vector database management. This allows developers to utilize Hermes-style memory, personas, and task routing while remaining within a unified terminal environment.

## esaradev/icarus-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/esaradev/icarus-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/esaradev/icarus-plugin
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 51
Self-memory and replacement models — remember your work, train your replacement

Icarus is a Hermes plugin designed to create self-improving agents through shared memory and automated model fine-tuning. It captures agent decisions, session outcomes, and structured notes into a markdown-based "fabric" directory that can be optionally managed via Obsidian. This data is then used to extract high-quality training pairs for fine-tuning cheaper replacement models via Together AI. The system includes a full pipeline for evaluating candidate models against base versions and switching to them once performance thresholds are met.

Highlights:
- Cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories
- Automated training data extraction with decision-quality tagging
- End-to-end pipeline for fine-tuning and evaluating replacement models

---

The Hermes Optimization Guide is a comprehensive technical resource and toolkit designed to help users deploy, secure, and scale Hermes Agent in production environments. It provides 24 detailed instructional parts alongside runnable artifacts, including opinionated configuration templates, installable skills, and a one-command VPS bootstrap script. The project enables advanced workflows such as durable Kanban orchestration, multi-platform integration across 20+ services, and cost-optimized model routing using providers like Anthropic, OpenAI, and local deployments.

- Thirteen installable skills and five opinionated configuration templates

## chigwell/skilldock.io

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/chigwell/skilldock.io
GitHub: https://github.com/chigwell/skilldock.io
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 50
Registry of reusable AI skills based on AgentSkills specification

SkillDock is a registry and SDK for managing reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It provides a Python client and CLI that interact with an OpenAPI-driven backend to facilitate the discovery, installation, and versioning of agentic capabilities. The system supports recursive dependency resolution, local skill verification, and a commerce layer for buying and selling skills via Stripe or TON. Developers can publish new skill releases by packaging local directories with metadata defined in Markdown frontmatter.

Highlights:
- OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI for skill management and discovery
- Recursive dependency resolution for local skill installation and uninstallation
- Integrated commerce flow supporting Stripe and TON blockchain payments

## howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness
GitHub: https://github.com/howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 46
Meta-harness for Hermes Agent — meta-optimization with arxiv paper reference

Hermes Agent Meta-Harness is a standalone outer-loop optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes agents on verifiable coding benchmarks. It treats the agent as an execution backend and searches for optimal harness configurations—the code governing context collection and retrieval—rather than modifying model weights. The system automates benchmark orchestration, archive analysis, and frontier tracking to identify high-performing harness candidates. It currently supports TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, providing structured mutation and search capabilities to refine agentic workflows.

Highlights:
- Optimizes harness code through an automated outer-loop search process
- Orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluation via Hermes Agent
- Tracks performance frontiers with structured candidate mutation and comparison

---

## black-forest-labs/skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/black-forest-labs/skills
GitHub: https://github.com/black-forest-labs/skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 44
Official FLUX image generation skills — prompting guidelines and API integration

This project provides official image generation skills for FLUX models, offering standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns following the agentskills.io specification. It functions as a technical resource for developers to implement text-to-image and image-to-image workflows through structured JSON prompting, hex color specification, and multi-reference editing. The repository includes a model selection guide for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 variants and can be integrated directly into Claude Code as a plugin. Users can access documented API patterns for asynchronous polling, webhook verification, and rate limit management.

Highlights:
- Standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 model families
- API integration patterns for polling, webhooks, and error handling
- Support for JSON structured prompting and precise hex color specification

---

## unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser
GitHub: https://github.com/unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 44
AI-native browser built for autonomous agent control via MCP

Vessel is an open-source, Chromium-based browser designed specifically for autonomous agent control rather than traditional human-centric browsing. It provides a persistent runtime with durable state and a visible UI that allows humans to audit, intervene, and redirect long-running agent workflows. The browser integrates with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to support external harnesses like Hermes Agent and includes built-in tools for page schema inference, action rollback, and agent-readable bookmarks. It is primarily optimized for Linux and macOS environments where agents act as the primary operators.

Highlights:
- Provides a human-visible supervisory UI for auditing autonomous agent actions
- Exposes browser control via MCP for external agent harnesses
- Maintains durable state with named sessions and annotated checkpoints

---

## raulvidis/hermes-android

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/raulvidis/hermes-android
GitHub: https://github.com/raulvidis/hermes-android
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 38
Android device control — bridge app + Python toolset for mobile automation

Hermes-android provides a remote control bridge that allows AI agents to interact with Android devices via a Kotlin-based mobile app and a Python toolset. The system uses a WebSocket relay to connect phones behind NAT to a server without requiring port forwarding or USB cables, utilizing a 6-character pairing code for authentication. It enables agents to perform 36 distinct actions including UI navigation, media control, and location retrieval through Android's AccessibilityService. This integration allows for autonomous mobile tasks such as sending SMS, managing notifications, and capturing screen recordings.

Highlights:
- Executes 36 tools for UI navigation, media, and system control
- Connects via WebSocket relay without requiring USB or port forwarding
- Uses AccessibilityService for screen reading and automated interaction

---

## Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory
GitHub: https://github.com/Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 34
Meta-skill plugin that watches workflows and auto-generates reusable skills

Skill Factory is a meta-skill for Hermes Agent that automates the creation of reusable skills by monitoring user workflows. It functions by tracking session patterns in the background and proposing structured skill definitions when it detects repeatable sequences. Users can generate both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds directly from these proposals via slash commands or natural language. This tool streamlines the expansion of the Hermes ecosystem by turning manual environment setups and development tasks into permanent, executable assets.

Highlights:
- Automatically detects and proposes reusable skills from active workflows
- Generates both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds
- Provides slash commands to manage, list, and save detected patterns

---

## plur-ai/plur

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/plur-ai/plur
GitHub: https://github.com/plur-ai/plur
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 31
Shared memory layer with open engram YAML format for multi-agent systems

PLUR is a persistent, local-first memory layer designed to synchronize agent corrections, preferences, and conventions across different tools and sessions. It stores knowledge as plain YAML "engrams" on disk and uses a hybrid BM25 and embedding-based retrieval system to inject relevant context into agent prompts. The system employs an ACT-R activation model where facts strengthen with use and naturally decay over time to prevent context clutter. It integrates directly with MCP-compatible tools, Claude Code, and Hermes Agent to automate the capture and recall of project-specific knowledge.

Highlights:
- Stores memory locally as open-format YAML engrams with zero API costs
- Uses ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale ones
- Provides MCP tools for automated learning and hybrid context injection

---

## keepnotes-ai/keep

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/keepnotes-ai/keep
GitHub: https://github.com/keepnotes-ai/keep
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 31
Reflective memory for AI agents

Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that enables long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media files—by summarizing, embedding, and organizing them into a navigable graph of "edge tags." The system automatically surfaces relevant past learnings and commitments during agent sessions to improve decision-making. It supports fully local operation via Ollama or MLX, as well as cloud-hosted and API-based configurations.

Highlights:
- Indexes codebases, documents, and media with automatic summarization and OCR
- Uses edge tags to create navigable, bidirectional relationships between notes
- Provides MCP and CLI interfaces for seamless agent tool integration

---

## runtimenoteslabs/gladiator

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/runtimenoteslabs/gladiator
GitHub: https://github.com/runtimenoteslabs/gladiator
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 29
Autonomous AI companies competing for GitHub stars — agent-vs-agent arena

Gladiator is an autonomous agent arena that pits two AI companies against each other to compete for GitHub stars on identical repositories. The system uses Paperclip for orchestration and Hermes Agent workers to execute tasks according to distinct corporate strategies: growth-focused Blitz versus quality-focused Craft. A real-time dashboard visualizes the competition through scoreboards, task boards, and audit trails. Upon completion, the project demonstrates cross-company skill transfer by merging the rival entities and their learned memories.

Highlights:
- Orchestrates 9 autonomous agents across two competing AI companies
- Uses Paperclip and Hermes Agent for task execution and learning
- Real-time dashboard visualizes agent tasks, code, and scoreboard metrics

---

## AxDSan/mnemosyne

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AxDSan/mnemosyne
GitHub: https://github.com/AxDSan/mnemosyne
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 29
The Zero-Dependency, Sub-Millisecond AI Memory System for Hermes Agents

Mnemosyne is a local-first memory system designed for the Hermes Agent framework to provide private, zero-dependency context management. It utilizes SQLite with native vector search and full-text search to store conversations and knowledge without requiring external databases or API keys. The system operates entirely in-process to eliminate network latency and supports offline use cases. It features a three-tier architecture consisting of working, episodic, and scratchpad memory for long-term information retention.

Highlights:
- Local-first storage using SQLite with native vector and full-text search
- Sub-millisecond latency for read, write, and search operations
- Native integration as a Hermes Agent memory provider plugin

---

17 specialized agents for research, planning, implementation, quality, and infrastructure

Hermes is a multi-agent orchestration system for OpenCode AI designed to automate complex software development lifecycles. It utilizes a master orchestrator to route tasks through specialized pipelines involving 17 distinct agents focused on research, planning, implementation, quality, and infrastructure. The system enforces mandatory quality gates, such as automated reviews and security audits, while maintaining state across session checkpoints. It integrates with MCP servers and supports various model providers to handle tasks ranging from feature development to performance optimization.

Highlights:
- Coordinates 17 specialized agents across six functional domains
- Implements strict pipeline architectures for features, fixes, and refactoring
- Enforces mandatory quality gates and security audits for code changes

## Euraika-Labs/pan-ui

Stars: 27
Self-hosted AI workspace with chat, skills, extensions, memory, profiles, and runtime controls

Pan is a self-hosted web dashboard and workspace designed specifically for the Hermes Agent runtime. It provides a centralized interface to manage agent interactions through a chat-first environment that supports SSE streaming and tool timelines. Users can discover and install skills from skills.sh, manage MCP integrations, and organize work into isolated profiles with unique memory and sessions. The system includes a setup wizard that automatically detects Hermes configurations and can run as a background daemon or system service.

Highlights:
- Chat-first workspace with real-time SSE streaming and tool approval cards
- Integrated marketplace for discovering and installing skills and MCP servers
- Isolated profile management for separate sessions, memory, and API keys

## ReinaMacCredy/maestro

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ReinaMacCredy/maestro
GitHub: https://github.com/ReinaMacCredy/maestro
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 25
Harness for long-running AI agents — structured memory, cross-feature learning, plan-approve-execute

Maestro is a local-first agent harness designed to coordinate long-running multi-agent software engineering workflows. It persists shared state, missions, and project context directly to the local filesystem in a .maestro directory rather than relying on external databases or chat history. This allows human operators to drive multiple terminal-based agent sessions, such as Hermes or Claude, while maintaining a disciplined and inspectable record of progress through structured handoffs and checkpoints.

Highlights:
- Local-first state management using on-disk .maestro directory and JSON snapshots
- Structured mission lifecycle with milestones, features, and validation assertions
- Native handoff launches that generate markdown briefs for fresh agent sessions

## Abruptive/Ankh.md

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Abruptive/Ankh.md
GitHub: https://github.com/Abruptive/Ankh.md
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 25
TAW Agent framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents

Ankh.md is an open-source framework designed to transform a single Hermes Agent into multiple specialized, project-specific instances. It works by detecting a local `.agent/` directory and dynamically overriding the default `hermes` command with a scoped agent configuration. This allows developers to maintain unique instructions, skills, and memories for different codebases or workflows without cross-project interference.

Highlights:
- Per-folder scoping with local configuration and skills
- Seamlessly switches between default and scoped Hermes agents
- Supports project-local memories, tools, and custom instructions

---

Stars: 25
Glassmorphic web interface for Hermes Agent — your self-hosted AI assistant

Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web dashboard designed to serve as the centralized command center for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It functions as a single-file React application served via a lightweight Python proxy, enabling users to interact with their self-hosted assistant without complex frontend deployments. The interface provides comprehensive visibility into agent operations through real-time log streaming, a task board for tracking active tool work, and integrated file browsing. It also features dedicated management views for MCP tools, cron jobs, and skill libraries to streamline agent orchestration.

Highlights:
- Single-file React architecture with real-time log streaming
- Integrated file browser with inline workspace previews
- Management interfaces for MCP tools, skills, and cron jobs

ExecPlan is an Agent Skill designed to help AI coding agents autonomously manage complex, long-running implementation tasks. It utilizes a structured execution plan that allows users to review and approve the approach before implementation starts. The system tracks progress in real-time and provides rollback procedures for safe recovery. It is compatible with tools such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Goose.

## roli-lpci/lintlang

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/roli-lpci/lintlang
GitHub: https://github.com/roli-lpci/lintlang
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 24
Static linter for AI agent configs, tool descriptions, and system prompts. HERM v1.1 scoring.

lintlang is a static analysis tool designed to identify structural and linguistic failures in AI agent configurations, system prompts, and tool definitions. It utilizes regex and AST-based heuristics rather than LLM calls to detect issues like vague tool descriptions, missing termination conditions, and schema mismatches. This approach ensures deterministic, zero-cost scans that run in approximately 2ms per file. The tool is intended for use in CI/CD pipelines to prevent agent looping and incorrect tool selection before runtime.

Highlights:
- Deterministic scans using static regex and structural heuristics
- Zero LLM calls for cost-free and offline CI integration
- Detects seven specific structural failure modes including H1-H7 patterns

---

## cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill
GitHub: https://github.com/cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 22
Progressive MCP client with three-layer lazy loading — validates agentskills.io pattern

Agentic-MCP-Skill is an experimental MCP client and daemon designed to optimize token usage through a three-layer progressive disclosure pattern. It functions by lazy-loading server metadata, tool lists, and specific schemas only when required by the agent, rather than loading all tool definitions upfront. This approach reduces context window overhead by up to 86% while providing a socket-based architecture for persistent server management. The project includes a CLI for managing MCP servers like Playwright and supports hot-reloading configurations without restarting the daemon.

Highlights:
- Three-layer lazy loading reduces token consumption for large toolsets
- Socket-based daemon maintains persistent connections to multiple MCP servers
- CLI supports metadata inspection, tool listing, and schema retrieval

---

## robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus
GitHub: https://github.com/robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 21
Multi-provider web search (Serper, Tavily, Exa, Querit, Perplexity) with auto-routing

Hermes Web Search Plus is a multi-provider search and extraction plugin designed to give Hermes Agent flexible access to live web data. It uses a capability-based routing system that automatically selects the best available tool based on the user's configured API keys, such as Tavily, Brave, or Firecrawl. The plugin provides three distinct tools for broad web searching, URL content extraction, and synthesized answers with citations. This modular approach ensures the agent remains functional with any single provider while scaling its capabilities as more keys are added.

Highlights:
- Supports 10 providers including Serper, Tavily, Exa, and Linkup
- Auto-routing logic selects providers based on query and key availability
- Provides dedicated tools for search, extraction, and cited answer synthesis

---

## 0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes
GitHub: https://github.com/0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 21
Migration tool from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent

openclaw-to-hermes is a migration utility designed to transition users from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automates the transfer of agent personas, workspace files, model configurations, and credentials between the two ecosystems. The tool addresses specific limitations found in earlier migration methods, such as incorrect authentication formatting and incomplete workspace detection. It provides a safe migration path through dry-run modes and automated backup creation.

Highlights:
- Migrates agent personas, memories, skills, and workspace files
- Maps complex OpenClaw model configurations to Hermes YAML format
- Handles credential transfers from local and system-level environment files

## armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge
GitHub: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 17
Converts repos, docs, and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant skills

Skill Forge is a development tool that converts source code, documentation, and developer discourse into verified instruction files for AI agents. It uses Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) analysis and documentation scraping to extract precise function signatures, parameter types, and usage patterns. Every generated instruction includes a provenance map linking back to specific file line numbers at pinned commits or source URLs to prevent model hallucinations. The tool supports lifecycle management for versioned skills and exports files compliant with the agentskills.io specification for use in agents like Cursor and Claude.

Highlights:
- Generates version-pinned AI instructions from codebases and documentation
- Provides verifiable audit trails with file-level and line-level citations
- Exports agentskills.io-compliant markdown and JSON for cross-platform agent use

---

## 42-evey/hermes-plugins

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/42-evey/hermes-plugins
GitHub: https://github.com/42-evey/hermes-plugins
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 16
Goal management, inter-agent bridge, model selection, and cost control plugins

Evey's Hermes Plugins is a collection of 23 custom extensions designed to enhance the autonomy, observability, and cost-efficiency of the Hermes Agent framework. The suite implements specialized logic for multi-model decision debates, smart task routing with fallback chains, and structured telemetry logging. These tools enable agents to manage their own goals, perform self-correction loops, and consolidate long-term memory into vector storage. Additionally, the project includes a bidirectional bridge for Claude Code and budget enforcement via Langfuse integration.

Highlights:
- Autonomous planning, reflection, and multi-model consensus debate tools
- Smart model routing with sensitivity filters and four-model fallback chains
- Automated memory consolidation into Qdrant vectors and identity evolution

## TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw
GitHub: https://github.com/TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 16
Hermes Agent sandboxed with hardware-level enforcement

HermesClaw is a deployment framework that runs the Hermes Agent within NVIDIA’s OpenShell sandbox to provide hardware-level security enforcement. It utilizes kernel-level mechanisms like Landlock LSM and Seccomp BPF to restrict network egress, filesystem access, and system calls. Users can manage the agent's capabilities through predefined security policies ranging from strict inference-only modes to permissive settings for web and messaging integration. The project includes a library of over 40 built-in tools and skills for tasks such as automated research, code review, and multi-platform messaging.

Highlights:
- Hardware-enforced sandboxing via NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level security layers
- Dynamic policy presets to control network, filesystem, and tool access
- Supports persistent memory and 40+ built-in skills for automated workflows

---

## amanning3390/hermeshub

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/amanning3390/hermeshub
GitHub: https://github.com/amanning3390/hermeshub
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 15
Community skill browsing, search, and one-click installation hub

HermesHub is a curated skills registry for Hermes Agent that provides a secure way to browse, install, and share AI agent capabilities. It utilizes the agentskills.io open standard and an automated security scanner that checks for threats like prompt injection and data exfiltration. The platform includes a creator marketplace supporting x402 and Micropayment protocols for premium skills, as well as an agent-to-agent feedback system for trust scoring.

Highlights:
- Automated security scanning with 65+ threat detection rules
- Creator marketplace with crypto and Stripe-based payment protocols
- Agent-to-agent feedback system for reliability and trust scores

## nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel
GitHub: https://github.com/nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel
Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 12
Hermes with integrated CaMeL trust boundaries for safer autonomous execution

This project is a version of the Hermes Agent that integrates CaMeL-style trust boundaries to protect against indirect prompt injection. It implements an opt-in runtime guard that monitors or enforces security policies by gating sensitive tool side effects against verified user intent. The system maintains the core Hermes features, including a self-improving learning loop, autonomous skill creation, and cross-platform accessibility via Telegram, Discord, and Slack. Users can toggle between monitoring modes to record policy decisions or enforcement modes to block unauthorized actions during autonomous execution.

Highlights:
- Opt-in CaMeL runtime guard for indirect prompt-injection protection
- Self-improving learning loop with autonomous skill creation and memory
- Multi-platform gateway supporting Telegram, Discord, Slack, and Signal

## 0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 11
Nix package and NixOS module for reproducible Hermes deployment

nix-hermes-agent provides a Nix package and NixOS module for the declarative deployment of Hermes Agent. It allows users to manage the entire agent lifecycle—including configuration, workspace documents, and systemd services—through a single Nix configuration file. The module ensures reproducibility by rendering settings into a structured CLI configuration while keeping sensitive API keys secure via external environment files. It supports both Nix-managed declarative skills and native Hermes CLI interactive skills, providing a stable foundation for production-ready agent infrastructure.

Highlights:
- Declarative management of agent config, documents, and systemd services
- Secure secrets handling via systemd EnvironmentFiles and sops-nix integration
- Hybrid skill support for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installs

## Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander

GitHub: https://github.com/Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 11
Autonomous SRE agent — detects, heals, and learns from production incidents

Hermes Incident Commander is an autonomous SRE agent designed to automate the detection, diagnosis, and remediation of production infrastructure failures. It utilizes the Hermes Agent framework to monitor system vitals, classify incident severity, and apply fixes across CPU, memory, disk, and service layers. Following resolution, the agent generates structured post-mortem reports and automatically creates new prevention skills to handle similar future incidents more efficiently. The system integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time alerting and uses cron scheduling for continuous health audits.

Highlights:
- Automates incident triage, root cause analysis, and remediation actions.
- Generates persistent prevention skills and post-incident reports automatically.
- Integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time P0 alerts.

Job Scout Agent is an automated job search tool designed to streamline the discovery and tracking of employment opportunities. The agent utilizes Hermes Agent capabilities to fetch live listings from job board APIs, score them against user-defined profiles, and perform company research. It organizes findings into structured local directories and maintains a CSV tracker for easy integration with Google Sheets. This system eliminates manual searching by providing a daily summary of relevant roles with direct application links.

Highlights:
- Fetches live job listings using browser-based API integration

- Maintains an automated CSV tracker for application management

## Codename-11/hermes-relay

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Codename-11/hermes-relay
GitHub: https://github.com/Codename-11/hermes-relay
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 10
Hermes Companion — Android app for Hermes agent platform. Chat, terminal, and device control over WSS.

Hermes-Relay is a companion suite that enables remote interaction with a server-deployed Hermes agent through a native Android app and a desktop CLI. It uses a WebSocket Secure (WSS) relay to bridge local devices with remote agent instances, allowing for persistent sessions across different platforms. The Android application provides mobile device control including screen reading and gesture execution, while the desktop client allows a remote agent to execute local terminal commands and file operations. This setup eliminates the need for SSH by routing tool execution and chat interfaces through a unified relay plugin.

Highlights:
- Native Android app for remote chat, voice, and device control
- Experimental desktop CLI for local terminal and file system access
- WSS relay plugin for seamless server-to-client tool execution

## rodmarkun/anihermes

GitHub: https://github.com/rodmarkun/anihermes
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 9
Local anime server and tracker via natural language for Hermes Agent

AniHermes is a natural language anime media server and tracker built on the Hermes Agent framework. It leverages LLM orchestration to manage downloads via qBittorrent, track series progress, and organize local libraries through conversational interfaces like Telegram, Discord, or CLI. The system utilizes agentic capabilities such as vision analysis for identifying anime from screenshots and automated CRON jobs for scheduling weekly episode downloads. It integrates directly with Anilist for watchlist synchronization and provides parallel search functionality across multiple sources like SubsPlease and Nyaa.

Highlights:
- Natural language control for downloads, tracking, and library management
- Vision-based anime identification from screenshots using Hermes Agent
- Automated weekly episode tracking via NL-defined CRON jobs

Autonomous monitoring — cron-based research ingestion, cost forecasting, headless systemd

This project is a reference implementation for building autonomous, long-running monitoring systems using Hermes Agent. It utilizes cron-based workflows to ingest AI research from arXiv and track infrastructure repositories on GitHub. The system automatically generates structured markdown reports covering compute risk analysis, cost projections, and trend dashboards. It serves as a template for developers to deploy headless, scheduled agents that transform raw research data into actionable infrastructure intelligence.

## teknium1/hermes-miniverse

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/teknium1/hermes-miniverse
GitHub: https://github.com/teknium1/hermes-miniverse
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 8
Bridge Hermes to Miniverse pixel worlds — agent embodiment in virtual environments

Hermes-miniverse is a bridge that enables Hermes Agents to inhabit and interact within Miniverse pixel worlds. It functions through a three-part architecture consisting of a standalone bridge daemon, a gateway hook for state broadcasting, and a specific skill that allows the agent to consciously navigate the environment. This integration allows agents to maintain a visual presence, update their status in real-time, and engage in bidirectional communication with other AI entities. Users can deploy multiple bridges simultaneously to populate a virtual world with specialized Hermes instances.

Highlights:
- Connects Hermes Agents to Miniverse pixel-based virtual environments
- Synchronizes live agent states including working, thinking, and idle
- Enables bidirectional messaging between Hermes and other world agents
